title: Sausage Traybake With Broccoli Salsa
summary: This one might look time-consuming, but it's just about getting all the bits into the oven. It’s super hands-off, and the result is a hearty comforting meal.

ingredients.label: ½ Small Bunch of Fresh Oregano (12.5g)
ingredients.name: Fresh Oregano

method.description: Trim and chop the aubergines and tomatoes into 2cm-sized chunks (leave any small tomatoes whole). Trim the broccoli and chop the stem into 1cm chunks. Discard the tough oregano stems, and thinly slice the garlic.

Add the chopped veg, broccoli stalks, oregano and garlic into an oven-proof dish with 2 tbsp of balsamic vinegar, 5 tbsp of olive oil, salt, and pepper. Toss it all together. Place the sausages on top. Roast for 35-40 mins, until the sausages are cooked and the veggies have softened.

Meanwhile, roughly chop the broccoli florets. Add the olives and parsley into a food processor and blitz until finely minced. Transfer into a bowl. Add the broccoli into the processor and blitz until finely minced. Add the minced broccoli to bowl of minced olives along with 70ml of olive oil and the remaining balsamic vinegar. Season with salt and pepper and mix. Set aside.
Drain the peppers and chop them into 2cm chunks.

Add the couscous into a heatproof bowl with 245ml of boiling water, season with plenty of freshly cracked black pepper. Cover tightly and set aside to rehydrate, 10-15 mins. Once ready, grate in the Parm, mix, taste and season with salt and pepper, if needed.
Stir the roasted peppers into the veg. Share the couscous between your plates, top with the sausage traybake and the broccoli green olive salsa.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
